record.field: Your orientation to the whole Neon ERP as Managing Director — how to see the business, approve what needs your sign-off, sell within the system as our top salesperson, and oversee and coach the sales team. Work each part against the live system.

field.name: html_content
field.p: Orient to what exists and where it lives. You don't operate every area, but as MD you should know the shape of the system:

ul.li: CRM / Sales — leads, pipeline, quotes (where you and the team sell).
Finance — quotes→invoicing, payments, bank statements, reports (Kudzaiishe's domain; you oversee disbursements).
Operations / Jobs — events, crew, equipment, the workshop (you're not involved in crew/equipment selection — this runs without you).
WhatsApp arm — the business runs a lot through WhatsApp: client intake, crew coordination, quote-by-WhatsApp, approvals (incl. yours).
Dashboards & Intelligence — your command centre + the sales-intelligence boards (Section 5).

field.p: ✅ Done when: you can name the major areas and what each is for.

field.name: html_content
field.p: Your dashboard is your daily business-at-a-glance.

ul.li: KPI tiles: Pipeline Value, New Leads, Hot Deals, Aging Quotes, Won This Month, Win Rate (90d). These tiles are team-wide — they show the whole company's numbers, not just yours.
Filter chips: All / Hot / Aging / Won.
Pipeline by Stage, Win Rate, Lead Sources.
Alerts — as a director, shows all reps' overdue invoices and stale quotes (a rep sees only their own). Your team-wide warning feed.
Hot Deals Watch, Aging Quotes — deals needing a push.
Tasks — your to-dos, including escalations routed to you (Section 5).
AI Insights + AI Copilot (chat) — ask questions, get narrative insights; the Copilot can log leads, move stages, update values (with a confirm step).

field.p: ✅ Done when: you can read your dashboard and know the tiles are team-wide.

record.field: You sell at mastery level — same workflow as the team

field.name: html_content
field.p: As our top salesperson you use the full sales workflow at mastery level — the same content the team learns. The steps below mirror the Sales course: capture leads, work the pipeline, log interactions, raise catalogue-priced quotes, confirm logistics before sending, find your quotes, the discount rule, and the culture standards.
✅ Done when: you can run the full sales workflow independently.

field.name: html_content
field.p: Every enquiry becomes a lead — if it's not in the CRM, it doesn't exist. Leads reach you two ways: WhatsApp enquiries auto-create a lead, and you manually create leads for enquiries by phone, email, or in person. Capture the client, contact, and what they need.

field.name: html_content
field.p: Your deals live on the Pipeline as cards. Keep each deal in the stage that reflects reality — the stages are New → Proposal Sent → Negotiation → Closed Won / Lost, plus Payment Pending Verification. Move cards by drag-and-drop on the kanban, or via the status bar inside the deal.

field.name: html_content
field.p: Open a deal and log what happened — a call summary, a meeting note, a scheduled follow-up activity. Same-day, every time. This is how the system and your team see a deal is being worked.

field.name: html_content
field.p: Raise a quote through the Neon quote flow. It prices from the live catalogue / pricing rules — not guesswork. It carries a currency (USD or ZWG, locked per quote) and moves through its states (Draft → Pending Approval → Approved → Sent to Client → Accepted). When a client accepts, it sets BOTH operations and finance in motion at once (the event starts being planned and finance picks up invoicing).

record.field: Confirm logistics before sending a quote

field.name: html_content
field.p: Before any quote goes to a client, confirm the logistics cost and include it (the LOGISTICS line). Get the figure from Operations and add it. A quote does not go out without logistics confirmed — acceptance triggers operations and finance at once, so a quote missing logistics becomes a margin problem instantly. This is a firm rule now and will become a built-in system check.

field.name: html_content
field.p: Your quotes live under My Quotation — the Neon quote list, where you find, reopen, and track your own (QUO-USD / QUO-ZIG numbered, with state and total).

field.name: html_content
field.p: A custom discount above the threshold needs MD approval — and as MD that sign-off is yours (see Section 4). Know the rule: reps can't apply an over-threshold discount without it.

ul.li: Respond to clients within 2 hours during business hours.
Formal English in all written client communication.
Never quote verbally — all pricing goes through an official Neon quote.
Send a proposal within 24 hours of a client meeting.
Follow up every proposal within 3 business days if no response.
Log every client interaction same-day in the CRM.
All client email correspondence copies the MD — the standard you set and uphold for the team (and model yourself).
Over-threshold discounts come to the MD for approval — that's you.
The promise every client should feel from Neon: Confidence, Clarity, Pride.

field.name: html_content
field.p: When a rep submits a quote you're notified by email or WhatsApp.

ul.li: Email: the notification links to the quote; review and approve/decline there.
WhatsApp (live): ask for a view of the quote, get a prompt to approve/decline, type your response.

field.p: Your approval is the gate between a drafted quote and one the client sees.

field.name: html_content
field.p: Over-threshold discounts come to you the same way (email or WhatsApp); reps can't apply them without your sign-off.

field.name: html_content
field.p: Disbursements flow from you. The full Undeposited-Funds module isn't in Odoo yet — you continue overseeing disbursements as you do now until it's built; when UF lands, this is where you'll do it.
✅ Done when: you can approve/decline a quote (email + WhatsApp) and a discount, and you understand your disbursement-oversight role.

field.name: html_content
field.p: Know which tools show live activity and which show historical patterns — the most important distinction in your oversight. Live tools, today:

ul.li: Native CRM grouped by Salesperson — open Pipeline / quote lists, group by salesperson → a live per-rep view of who's working what. Generic Odoo, but real and live. This is your live per-rep tool — there isn't a bespoke per-rep scoreboard yet.
Director dashboard — team-wide totals + the alert feed (all reps' overdue invoices & stale quotes).
Your escalation queue (Tasks) — deals 7 days with no movement, and un-followed-up quotes, escalate as to-dos to you. Your early-warning queue.
Feedback Insights (/neon/insights, manager-only) — live client-satisfaction timelines, sentiment, recurring-concern flags. Your client-health coaching tool.

p.em: Live data grows from cutover — as the team works post-1-July, these views fill with real activity.

record.field: D5.2 · Historical coaching material (pattern-spotting, NOT live scorekeeping)

ul.li: Win/Loss & Realisation by rep — historical win rates / realisation per salesperson.
Client Intelligence — per-client value, win rate, segment.
Demand & Seasonality — when business comes, recurring events.

p.em: These read the pre-cutover archive. They won't reflect live post-July performance until rebuilt on live data — a planned build. Use them to coach on patterns and learn from history.

field.name: html_content
field.p: Quote sign-off and discount sign-off (Section 4) are real, live manager controls — your levers on what reaches a client and at what price.

record.field: D5.4 · What the system does NOT yet track — coach these directly

field.name: html_content

ul.li: First-response time — the system flags first response at 2 hours (matching the standard), but per-lead only, retroactively, with no manager rollup / breach view per rep. You can't yet see "who's slow" at a glance — coach it; a manager-view build is planned.
"Copy the MD on all client emails" — not system-monitored; honour-based (tracking build planned).
Logistics-confirmed-before-quote — not yet a system gate (planned build).
Soft standards (never quote verbally, formal English, same-day logging) — coaching / spot-check.

field.p: ✅ Done when: you know your live oversight tools, your historical coaching boards, your approval controls, and which standards you coach directly because the system doesn't track them yet.

record.field: The whole team's numbers — the tiles are team-wide
Correct — the tiles are team-wide, not your own deals.
A rep submits a quote needing approval. How can you approve it?
Only by logging into a computer
By email (link to the quote) or WhatsApp (ask for a view, reply to approve/decline)
Correct — approve by email or over WhatsApp.
You want a live, per-rep view of who's working what. Where?
Native CRM Pipeline / quotes, grouped by Salesperson
Correct — native CRM grouped by salesperson is your live per-rep view.
The Win/Loss-by-rep and Client Intelligence boards show…
Historical patterns from before cutover — for coaching and learning, not live scorekeeping
Correct — these read the pre-cutover archive; coaching, not live scorekeeping.
The system tracks each rep's 1-hour response compliance for you.
True — there's a per-rep breach dashboard
False — first-response flags at 2 hours, per-lead, with no manager rollup yet; you coach response time directly
Correct — 2h, per-lead, no manager rollup; coach it directly.
True — the AI Copilot reports it hourly
Disbursements / Undeposited Funds oversight in Odoo today —
Fully built; you manage UF in the system now
The UF module isn't in Odoo yet; you oversee disbursements as you do now until it's built
Correct — UF isn't in Odoo yet; oversee disbursements as you do now.

field.name: html_content
field.p: Completed by a director, confirming Munashe can read the director dashboard, approve quotes and discounts (email + WhatsApp), run the full sales workflow, and oversee/coach the team using the live tools (native grouped CRM, alert feed, Feedback Insights, escalation queue) and historical boards — and that she knows which standards the system doesn't yet track and must be coached directly.
When marked complete, Director onboarding is signed off and recorded.
